heroHeadline: CRISPR Gene Therapy: From Laboratory to Clinic
heroSubtitle: After 14 years from discovery to approval, Casgevy — the world's first CRISPR therapy — is treating sickle cell patients. Base editing and prime editing advance toward the next generation of precision medicine.
footerNote: Data sourced from FDA, EMA, MHRA, NIH, peer-reviewed journals (Nature, Science, NEJM, Cell), ClinicalTrials.gov, and major science outlets. Clinical data reflects published peer-reviewed results only.
